title: Bridge Paper: Religious and spiritual experiences from a neuroscientific and complex systems perspective.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nonordinary Rendering** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Psychedelics — DMN disruption, ego dissolution, altered states, predictive processing decoupling.
**Phase context:** psychedelic, psilocybin, DMT, ego dissolution, ayahuasca, altered states, default mode network

Paper: Religious and spiritual experiences from a neuroscientific and complex systems perspective.
DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2025.106319
Abstract: The emergence of spiritual and religious practices can be seen as a major cognitive transition in the evolution of biological organisms. Spiritual and religious experiences are closely linked to evolved complex brain structures, as well as their dynamics and plasticity. In this review, we will focus on the neural mechanisms that make such experiences possible.
